meta.title: The Heart-Base, Bhavaṅga, and the Stages of Insight
meta.subtitle: A three-tier reading (Sutta / Abhidhamma / Commentary) of where the seat of mind, the life-continuum, and the insight-ladder come from.
meta.note: Companion to the individual-guidance census. Corpus claims are verbatim-grounded by direct fetch; every id resolves to a real corpus row. Abhidhamma-status and modern-practice material is secondary or attributed, marked as such.
meta.version_note: v1.1 (2026-06-15): resolved the three §10 partial cells with per-row verbatim grounding — the citta-vīthi via the Atthasālinī (As §79, "bhavaṅge āvaṭṭite vīthicittāni uppajjanti"); the Vibhaṅga analytical set completed with the Dhātuvibhaṅga (vb3); the Paṭis Ñāṇakathā confirmed to name the udayabbaya/bhaṅga ñāṇas.

sutta.text: Silent on any material seat of mind; the nearest seed is the luminous mind (pabhassara).

abhidhamma.text: An anonymous material base, "yaṁ rūpaṁ" (Paṭṭhāna); the mind-element's own base left indeterminate (Dhammasaṅgaṇī).

commentary.text: Names it the heart-base (hadaya-vatthu) and locates it in the heart's blood.

sutta.text: Absent; nearest analogue is again the luminous mind.

abhidhamma.text: Present as a bare term, a real relatum in the Paṭṭhāna's conditional relations.

commentary.text: Builds the full cognitive series (citta-vīthi) on the heart: when the life-continuum is turned, the cognitive-process cittas arise (bhavaṅge āvaṭṭite vīthicittāni uppajjanti).

abhidhamma.text: Not a temperament; the word means kamma (Vibhaṅga §817). The three roots are the akusalamūla.

para-canon.text: A three-fold root-carita with the foulness / love / dependent-origination remedy matrix.

commentary.text: The six-fold personality typology, the affinity theory, the carita→object matrix (Vism III §46), the heart-blood diagnostics (Vibh-a §70.58).
No structural addition to the roots themselves.

sutta.text: Not as a named graded ladder.

abhidhamma.text: Absent: zero hits across the seven books (negative controls).

para-canon.text: First defined, by name, with their own niddesa sections (udayabbayānupassanā- and bhaṅgānupassanā-ñāṇa appear in the Ñāṇakathā).

commentary.text: Systematized and refined (e.g. the momentary-vs-continuity reading of rise-and-fall).

abhidhamma.text: Not the source of the skeleton.

commentary.text: Populates and stages the bare list into the graded path.

abhidhamma.text: Canonical source: the Vibhaṅga's analytical chapters, one per category (khandha, āyatana, dhātu, paṭiccasamuppāda).

commentary.text: Deployed as the ground (bhūmi) the insight ladder dissects.
